# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Test

```bash
swift build                          # Build debug
swift build -c release               # Build release
swift test                           # Run all tests
swift test --filter McpServerTests   # Run a specific test class
swift test --filter testCreateSession # Run a specific test method
```

The compiled binary is output to `.build/debug/WebHost` and also copied to the repo root as `./WebHost`.

## Run

```bash
make run                             # Build release + create & launch WebHost.app
make app                             # Build + create WebHost.app (without launching)
make clean                           # Remove app bundle + build artifacts
open WebHost.app                     # Launch existing .app bundle
pkill -f WebHost && sleep 1 && make run  # Restart
curl http://localhost:9223/health     # Health check
```

Port is configurable via command line arg or `WEBHOST_PORT` env var (default: 9223).

The app runs as a macOS .app bundle (`WebHost.app`) with an LSUIElement agent (menu bar only, no dock icon), an optional window, and an HTTP server.

## Architecture

This is a macOS native app that implements an **MCP (Model Context Protocol) HTTP server** using AppKit + WebKit. It enables AI agents to control a real browser (WKWebView) through JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP.

### Stack
- **SnapKit** layout engine for Auto Layout constraints
- HTTP server uses `NWListener`/`NWConnection` (Network.framework), no SwiftNIO
- Concurrency via `NSLock` + `DispatchQueue.main.sync` for main-thread-bound WKWebView/NSWindow operations

### Source Layout

```
Sources/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.swift                 # Entry: NSApplication, status bar, NWListener HTTP server
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Server/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ McpServer.swift        # JSON-RPC handler: routes tools/initialize/list/call, manages sessions
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Browser/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ WebKitSession.swift    # WKWebView wrapper: multi-tab, screenshot, JS eval, interactive mode, event buffer
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ BlockerView.swift      # NSView subclass that consumes all mouse/key events (visual overlay)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ThemeAwareView.swift   # NSView subclass with appearance change callback (light/dark mode)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Model/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Event.swift            # Event struct + factory helpers (web/console, web/navigation, etc.)
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ JsonRpc.swift          # JSON-RPC 2.0 types: request, response, result, error, AnyCodable
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Session.swift          # Viewport struct only
Makefile                       # Build targets: all, build, app, run, clean
Info.plist.tmpl                # Info.plist template (app bundle metadata)
```

### Key Design Decisions

- **WKWebView must be created/accessed on main thread** ‚Äî all session operations dispatch to `DispatchQueue.main.sync`. `McpServer.handleSync` is synchronous and blocking via semaphores.
- **Interactive mode** ‚Äî when disabled, an `NSEvent` local monitor blocks mouse/key events targeting the browser view, and a `BlockerView` visual overlay + semi-transparent background indicates non-interactive state (yellow border). When enabled, the monitor passes events through and the border turns green.
- **Multi-tab** ‚Äî each `WebKitSession` has a primary tab + dynamically created tabs. Popups open as new tabs. The "main" tab cannot be closed.
- **Event buffer** ‚Äî capped at 1000 events. Events are timestamped and retrievable via `since=` parameter for reconnection.
- **MCP protocol** ‚Äî JSON-RPC 2.0 over raw HTTP. Tools are defined in `McpServer.toolDefinitions` and dispatched via `handleToolCall`. Standard flow: initialize ‚Üí tools/list ‚Üí tools/call ‚Üí stream events.
- **Theme switching** ‚Äî `ThemeAwareView` observes `viewDidChangeEffectiveAppearance()` to update layer colors (URL container border/background, tab backgrounds) when the system light/dark mode changes.
- **URL bar** ‚Äî shows domain only by default; displays full URL when the user clicks or starts editing. Single-line with ellipsis overflow.
- **Keyboard shortcuts** ‚Äî `Cmd+T` new tab, `Cmd+W` close active tab (maintains main tab).

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| `POST` | `/mcp/call` | JSON-RPC tool call |
| `GET` | `/mcp/stream?sessionId=&since=` | Event stream (polling) |
| `GET` | `/mcp/sessions` | List sessions |
| `DELETE` | `/mcp/sessions/{id}` | Close session |
| `GET` | `/health` | Health check |

### Tools (16 total)

Session lifecycle: `web_session_create`, `web_session_close`, `web_capabilities`
Navigation: `page_open`, `script_run`, `page_screenshot`, `go_back`, `go_forward`
Interaction: `web_set_interactive`, `web_inject`, `web_wait`, `web_dialog_response`
Tab management: `tab_list`, `tab_switch`, `tab_close`, `tab_create`

### Events

Generated by WKUIDelegate/WKNavigationDelegate callbacks: `web/console`, `web/navigation`, `web/error`, `web/dialog`, `web/popup`, `web/ping`

### Thread Safety

- `McpServer.sessions` is guarded by `NSLock`
- `WebKitSession.eventBuffer` uses `NSLock` internally
- All WKWebView/NSWindow access must happen on main thread (enforced via `dispatchPrecondition`)
- Session cleanup on close must occur before dictionary removal to prevent delegate callbacks on dealloc
